titleStmt.title: Letter from Michael Citroen to Herbert Samuel, 12 May 1916.
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Michael Citroen

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Michael Citroen (b.1892), a British seed and plant merchant, to Herbert Samuel (1870-1963), the Home Secretary for England. The letter is an application for compensation by Michael Citroen. In the letter he states that he went to Dublin to attend the Spring Show, sending his last £50 to get there in hope of receiving a profit, however due to the Easter Rising he could not attend and lost his money. He goes on to state that this has effectively ruined him. Citroen goes on to ask for a government position if he cannot be compensated.

msIdentifier.collection: National Archive of Ireland-Chief Secretary's Office-Registered Papers, NAI/CSO/RP/1916/5611/8203/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Phone 1613 Chiswick MICHAEL RAINS, Ltd., (Patronised by Royalty,) BULB GROWERS, Seed and Plant Merchants, Horticultural Sundriesmen. _____________________ Established 1856. _____________________ Telegraphic Address - deleted text 54 Chiswick Lane, London, W. May 12th 1916. The Home Secretary Whitehall, S.W. Dear Sir, I am given to understand that some
compensation will be given to sufferers of the Irish
rebellion. I beg to inform you that I went to the Dublin
Spring Show with three assistants, but on account of the
unfortunate circumstances, we could not reach the Show.
This affair cost me nearly Â£50. I would not mind this
loss so much, but that it was the last few pounds
I had in the world. I have been attending this show
for some years & always made a considerable profit.
I might mention that through prevailing circumstances
my business as above has been practically ruined, I suffered
considerable loss the first & second year, & now I am left with
nothing. If nothing can be done for me in this matter,
may I ask you, if something could be found for me
in some Government office. I am above the Military
age, & have had considerable experience.
Trusting to receive an early & favourable reply. I am,
Your obedient Servant
Michael Citroen Ackn.2 & say
that no measures are in
contemplation wh. wd. meet the
case he adduces RC 16/5/16
The Home Secretary's Compliment Slip With The Private Secretary's compliments
Envelope Postmarked CHISWICK W 3 PM 12 MAY 16 Private & Personal The Home Secretary Whitehall S.W. Irish Office
